paper_title: Material and Methods of Cooksonia barrandei Fossil Study

methods.description: Process of collecting and identifying Cooksonia barrandei fossils and associated specimens from historical and new localities.

steps.description: The fossils consist of the type material of Cooksonia barrandei and three more fertile specimens from the Loděnice locality.
steps.searchable_summary: collect Cooksonia barrandei fossils from Loděnice locality

steps.description: We relocated the original locality where Barrande collected the specimens of C. barrandei and all the present material we examined here including three other fertile specimens.
steps.searchable_summary: relocate original collection site of Barrande for C. barrandei

steps.description: Eleven sterile fragments of cooksonioids have been newly collected from the brown-grey calcareous and tuffaceous shale of the belophorus Biozone.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sterile cooksonioid fragments from belophorus Biozone

methods.description: Microscopic examination and documentation of plant fossils using various microscopy techniques.

steps.description: Plant fossils were studied and documented using an Olympus SZX-12 binocular microscope equipped with a DP-72 digital camera.
steps.searchable_summary: use Olympus SZX-12 microscope for fossil documentation

steps.description: Specimens were photographed immersed in alcohol under diffuse polarised light using a Canon EOS 50D digital camera equipped with a Sigma Macro 50 lens and circular polarizer filter.
steps.searchable_summary: photograph specimens with Canon EOS 50D under polarised light

steps.description: Plant microfossils were studied and documented using a NIKON BX51 light microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: use NIKON BX51 microscope for microfossil study

steps.description: Plants were also observed in low-vacuum mode of the Hitachi S-3700N SEM, but cellular details (e.g. conducting tissues) were not observed.
steps.searchable_summary: observe plants with Hitachi S-3700N SEM in low-vacuum mode

methods.description: Chemical maceration of spores and preparation of palynological slides for microscopic analysis.

steps.description: Dispersed and in situ spores were macerated using hydrochloric acid (24 hours), hydrofluoric acid (5 days), hydrochloric acid (15 minutes), potassium hydroxide (15 minutes) and washed by water several times.
steps.searchable_summary: macerate spores using acid and base treatments

steps.description: Palynological slides were prepared using a glycerine jelly mounting medium.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare palynological slides with glycerine jelly
